title: All Quiet on the Western Front
writer: Ian Stokell, Lesley Paterson, Edward Berger
logline: A young German soldier named Paul Bäumer experiences the brutal realities of World War I from his idealistic enlistment through the devastating final moments before the Armistice takes effect.
antagonistOrConflict: The dehumanizing machinery of war itself
themes: The disconnect between political decisions and battlefield reality

actBreaks.description: Paul and his fellow recruits arrive at the front-line trenches at Latierre Hill, transitioning from training to actual combat.

validation.ruling: Paul and the recruits arrive at the front-line trench where they meet the gaunt, hollow-eyed veterans and begin bailing muddy water - this irreversibly transforms them from enthusiastic recruits to soldiers facing the harsh reality of trench warfare.

actBreaks.description: General Friedrichs learns of the Armistice and decides to launch one final, pointless attack despite the war's end.

validation.ruling: The original scene 113 shows General Friedrichs receiving Armistice news, but scene 116 better represents the act break as Paul and Kat attempt their final dangerous mission to steal food from the farm, setting up the tragic final sequence that leads to Paul's death.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This is a relentlessly linear descent into hell that mirrors the historical progression of WWI itself. The script opens with the mechanical recycling of dead soldiers' uniforms—immediately establishing war as an industrial process that consumes young men. The structure follows Paul's journey from idealistic schoolboy to traumatized survivor, but it's really about the systematic destruction of humanity by institutional forces. The parallel diplomatic track with Erzberger negotiating the Armistice creates devastating irony—while politicians debate terms in train cars, soldiers continue dying in trenches. The script's genius lies in how it builds toward the Armistice not as salvation, but as the setup for its most tragic sequence.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing accelerates dramatically after scene 78 when the Armistice negotiations begin, creating mounting tension between the diplomatic timeline and the battlefield reality. The final act (scenes 113-153) compresses into a breathless 40-scene sprint covering just hours, as Friedrichs' final attack unfolds against the ticking clock toward 11 AM. Short, punchy scenes in the climax contrast with the longer, more contemplative sequences in Acts 1 and 2.

incitingIncident.description: Paul excitedly presents his draft notice to an Army Recruiter while students line up in underwear to receive uniforms for war.

validation.ruling: Paul receives his military uniform and draft papers from the Army Recruiter, officially enlisting for war and shattering his ordinary world as a student - this irreversible moment forces him into the main conflict and establishes the stakes of survival in WWI.

midpoint.description: Paul takes cover behind an earth berm, pulls a hand grenade pin, tosses it into the enemy trench killing French soldiers, then charges forward firing and screaming.
midpoint.sceneHeading: 84 EXT. LATIERRE / NO MAN'S LAND - DUSK 84

validation.ruling: Lieutenant Hoppe gives the order 'Soldiers, march' and Paul clutches his rifle before climbing out of the trench - this is the point of no return where Paul shifts from reactive survival to proactive combat, fundamentally changing his role from defensive to offensive action.

climax.description: Major Grenier orders a charge as the French jump from their destroyed trench to engage in hand-to-hand combat.

validation.ruling: Paul engages in brutal hand-to-hand combat, fighting with bayonets and rifle butts in apocalyptic melee - this represents the peak confrontation and highest dramatic tension as Paul faces the ultimate test of survival in direct combat with enemy soldiers.

scenes.heading: 1 EXT. WOODS, FRANCE 1917 - DAWN 1
scenes.summary: A peaceful creek flows through woods in France at dawn, with pine cones falling and distant thunder rumbling ominously.

scenes.heading: 2 INT. FOX DEN - DAY 2
scenes.summary: A vixen feeds her young cubs in a fox den as distant thunder and tremors shake dirt from the ceiling above them.

scenes.heading: 3 EXT. LATIERRE / TRENCH - DAY 3
scenes.summary: German soldiers huddle in trenches under devastating artillery bombardment, with shells exploding and bodies flying through the air.

scenes.heading: 4 EXT. BEHIND GERMAN LINES / MASS GRAVE - DUSK 4
scenes.summary: Soldiers unload dead German recruits from a truck at a mass grave, stripping Heinrich Gerber's bloody uniform from his corpse.

scenes.heading: 5 EXT. BEHIND GERMAN LINES / TRAIN TRACKS - DAY 5
scenes.summary: Dead soldiers' uniforms are loaded into a freight train boxcar alongside wounded soldiers.

scenes.heading: 6 EXT. TEXTILE FACTORY, GERMANY - DAY 6
scenes.summary: A truck delivers bundles of military uniforms to a textile factory in Germany.

scenes.heading: 7 INT. TEXTILE FACTORY WASH ROOM, GERMANY - DAY 7
scenes.summary: Uniforms are dumped into hot soapy water that turns red with blood as a woman stirs the vat.

scenes.heading: 8 INT. TEXTILE FACTORY DRYING ROOM, GERMANY - DAY 8
scenes.summary: A washerwoman collects half-dry, washed uniforms hanging from a drying line.

scenes.heading: 9 INT. TEXTILE FACTORY HALL, GERMANY - DAY 9
scenes.summary: Uniforms are wheeled down a factory hall in a hand cart.

scenes.heading: 10 INT. TEXTILE FACTORY, GERMANY - DAY 10
scenes.summary: A young seamstress examines and repairs a uniform jacket with a stab wound, the collar reading 'Heinrich Gerber.'

scenes.heading: 11 EXT. COUNTRY ROAD, GERMANY - DAY 11
scenes.summary: Paul Bäumer bicycles along a country road and is passed by a truck carrying uniforms and a disfigured veteran.

scenes.heading: 12 EXT. SMALL TOWN / ALLEY - DAY 12
scenes.summary: Paul rides his bicycle through a small town.

scenes.heading: 13 EXT. SCHOOL - DAY 13
scenes.summary: Paul arrives at school and meets his friends Kropp, Müller, and Behm, who groan when Paul shakes his head in resignation.

scenes.heading: 14 INT. SCHOOL / STRAIRS - DAY 14
scenes.summary: Students crowd the school staircase holding marching orders while the Principal gives a fiery speech about becoming men and going to war.

scenes.heading: 15 INT. SCHOOL / HALLS - DAY 15
scenes.summary: The Principal's voice echoes through empty school halls as he warns about doubt in the darkest hours before attack.

scenes.heading: 16 INT. SCHOOL / CLASSROOM - DAY 16
scenes.summary: The Principal's speech continues through empty classrooms, declaring that doubt and hesitation are treason to the homeland.

scenes.heading: 17 INT. SCHOOL / CELLAR - DAY 17
scenes.summary: The Principal's voice reaches the cellar where a tired veteran with a smashed face smokes a cigarette.

scenes.heading: 18 INT. SCHOOL / STAIRS - DAY 18
scenes.summary: The Principal concludes his speech, promising the boys will march on Paris and declaring them Germany's Greatest Generation.

scenes.heading: 19 INT. SCHOOL / AUDITORIUM - DAY 19
scenes.summary: Paul excitedly presents his draft notice to an Army Recruiter while students line up in underwear to receive uniforms for war.
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul officially enlists for war, setting the main story in motion

scenes.heading: 20 INT. SCHOOL / STOREROOM - DAY 20
scenes.summary: Paul puts on his new military uniform in a storeroom while Ludwig Behm stands beside him showing visible doubts.

scenes.heading: 21 INT. SMALL TOWN / CHURCH TOWER - DAY 21
scenes.summary: Church bells ring loudly in the belfry as a grand send-off.

scenes.heading: 22 EXT. SMALL TOWN / CHURCH SQUARE - DAY 22
scenes.summary: The church square sits mostly empty except for a few women, children, and old men, with young recruits visible in the distance.

scenes.heading: 23 EXT. SMALL TOWN / STREET - DAY 23
scenes.summary: The recruits march out of town singing while Müller grins and waves at girls among the onlookers.

scenes.heading: 24 EXT. POPLAR-LINED AVENUE, GERMANY - DAY 24
scenes.summary: The recruits march down a poplar-lined avenue singing as their voices carry over the fields.

scenes.heading: 25 EXT. TROOP VAN, WESTERN FRONT - DAY 25
scenes.summary: Lieutenant Hoppe inspects the recruits' rifles in a troop transport truck, finding Kropp's weapon dirty and berating him.

scenes.heading: 26 EXT. FIELD HOSPITAL - DAY 26
scenes.summary: An Army Doctor commandeers the recruits' truck for wounded soldiers at an overcrowded field hospital, forcing them to march.

scenes.heading: 27 EXT. MUDDY ROAD TO FRONT - DAY 27
scenes.summary: The recruits march toward the front as shells explode nearby, causing most to hit the dirt while Hoppe screams gas alerts.

scenes.heading: 28 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / ACCESS TRENCH - DAY 28
scenes.summary: Lieutenant Hoppe leads the exhausted recruits through an access trench as shells explode and bullets zing overhead.

scenes.heading: 29 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / TRENCH - DAY 29
scenes.summary: The recruits reach their unit at the front and are immediately ordered to bail muddy water from the waterlogged trenches.
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul and his friends arrive at the brutal reality of trench warfare

scenes.heading: 30 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / TRENCH - NIGHT 30
scenes.summary: Paul and Kropp pull guard duty in the cold, wet trench at night while Paul cleans his rifle and listens to rustling sounds.

scenes.heading: 31 INT. LATIERRE HILL / BUNKER - NIGHT 31
scenes.summary: Soldiers huddle in a bunker during intense shelling as Behm panics and wants to flee, but Paul holds him back.

scenes.heading: 32 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / UNDERGROUND - DAWN 32
scenes.summary: Paul lies buried under dirt after the bombardment, gasping for air until Müller and other soldiers dig him out.

scenes.heading: 33 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / DESTROYED TRENCH - DAWN 33
scenes.summary: Paul is freed from the debris and surveys the completely destroyed trench while Kat sits eating bread nearby.

scenes.heading: 34 EXT. FIELDS - DAY 34
scenes.summary: Tall golden wheat sways peacefully in a field.

scenes.heading: 35 EXT. FIELDS BEHIND THE FRONT - DAWN 35
scenes.summary: Bare autumn fields stretch under low fog as a title card indicates 18 months have passed to November 1918.
scenes.turningPointNote: Major time jump showing Paul's survival and the war's progression

scenes.heading: 36 INT/EXT. TRUCK / ROAD BY FARM - DAY 36
scenes.summary: Paul and Kat jump off an ammunition truck and pay the driver with cigarettes before walking across a field.

scenes.heading: 37 EXT. ROAD / FARM - DAY 37
scenes.summary: Kat jokes about conquering France in 180 years while he and Paul approach a farm wall.

scenes.heading: 38 EXT. FARMYARD - DAY 38
scenes.summary: Kat climbs over a farmyard wall to steal food while Paul keeps watch, then both flee when a farmer shoots at them.

scenes.heading: EXT. STAGING CAMP - DAY 42A
scenes.summary: A covered truck rolls past a staging camp on a wide meadow as the sun sets low.

scenes.heading: 43 EXT. STAGING AREA CAMP / STABLE - DAY 43
scenes.summary: Soldiers peel potatoes at a staging camp while Kat tells a joke about war medals, then they spot three girls passing by.

scenes.heading: 44 OMITTED 44
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 45 EXT. FIELD POST OFFICE / STAGING AREA CAMP - DAY 45
scenes.summary: Mail bags are unloaded at the field post office while soldiers crowd around to collect their letters.

scenes.heading: 46 EXT. MEADOW / STAGING AREA CAMP - DAY 46
scenes.summary: Paul reads aloud a letter from Kat's wife describing a food package she's sending while both men sit on latrine boxes.

scenes.heading: 47 EXT. STAGING AREA CAMP BEHIND THE FRONT LINES - NIGHT 47
scenes.summary: Müller returns alone to the dark barracks at night.

scenes.heading: 48 INT. BARRACKS / STAGING AREA CAMP - NIGHT 48
scenes.summary: Paul questions Müller about his encounter with a French girl named Eloise, sharing her scarf's scent with his friends.

scenes.heading: 49 INT/EXT. DIPLOMAT'S LIMOUSINE / SCHOOL - DAY 49
scenes.summary: A convoy of diplomatic limousines with white flags passes a school where soldiers bury hundreds of coffins.

scenes.heading: 50 EXT. WOODS / ROAD - DAY 50
scenes.summary: The soldiers watch the diplomatic convoy pass and joke about saluting the Kaiser while crossing into the woods.

scenes.heading: 51 EXT. WOODS - DAY 51
scenes.summary: Paul and Kat walk through blasted woods where a dead soldier hangs naked in tree branches, missing both arms.

scenes.heading: 52 EXT. TRAIN YARD BEHIND FRONT LINES - DAY 52
scenes.summary: The squad reaches a destroyed train station and sits around a flatbed wagon while Tjaden complains about turnip sandwiches.

scenes.heading: 53 INT. TRAIN YARD / ENGINE HALL - DAY 53
scenes.summary: Kat pushes open a gate to a dark train hall and the men split up with flashlights while Paul slips outside alone.

scenes.heading: 54 INT. TRAIN YARD / BETWEEN TWO HALLS - DAY 54
scenes.summary: Paul moves through a dark alley between storehouses, hears noises, cocks his rifle, and discovers the source is gaunt birds pecking at a dead dog on a chain.

scenes.heading: 55 EXT. TRAIN YARD BEHIND FRONT LINES - DAY 55
scenes.summary: Albert sits with the squad eating sandwiches, finds a poster of a girl in a summer dress by the ocean, cuts away the gentleman beside her with his knife, and pockets the picture.

scenes.heading: 56 INT. TRAIN YARD / WAREHOUSE - DAY 56
scenes.summary: Paul explores an abandoned warehouse with scattered belongings, reaches a rusted door, listens to silence, then opens the bolt and pushes through with his rifle barrel.

scenes.heading: 57 INT. TRAIN YARD / STOREROOM - DAY 57
scenes.summary: Paul enters a dark, quiet storeroom where flies buzz around a sticky pile in the foreground as his breath forms in the damp air and fear rises in his eyes.

scenes.heading: 58 INT. TRAIN YARD / ENGINE HALL - DAY 58
scenes.summary: Franz hears Paul shouting for Kat in the distance, turns and meets up with Kat from a side wing, then they head down the hall together.

scenes.heading: 59 INT. TRAIN YARD / STOREROOM - DAY 59
scenes.summary: Franz, Kat, and Paul discover sixty dead recruits on the storeroom floor with blue faces and black lips, their gas masks in their lifeless hands, all killed by gas after removing their masks too soon.

scenes.heading: 60 EXT. TRAIN YARD BEHIND FRONT LINES - DAY 60
scenes.summary: Outside in fresh air, a surviving kid vomits blood and collapses as Kat tries to help him drink water and walk to the infirmary, but the boy is too weak and pale.

scenes.heading: 61 EXT. EQUISAC MANOR - NIGHT 61
scenes.summary: A diplomatic limousine arrives at a mansion where General Friedrichs exits and takes a dog from a waiting boy.

scenes.heading: 62 EXT. EQUISAC MANOR - NIGHT 62
scenes.summary: General Friedrichs strides through the courtyard past smoking officers who salute him.

scenes.heading: 63 INT. EGUISAC MANOR / HALL - NIGHT 63
scenes.summary: General Friedrichs leads the dog down a hall while soldiers snap to attention and salute.

scenes.heading: 64 INT. EGUISAC MANOR / STUDY - NIGHT 64
scenes.summary: General Friedrichs enters his study headquarters, lights a cigarette, and receives a report from Major von Brixdorf about French forces advancing and an expected attack.

scenes.heading: 65 EXT. DIPLOMATIC TRAIN - NIGHT 65
scenes.summary: A diplomatic train's lights emerge from the darkness.

scenes.heading: 66 INT. DIPLOMATIC TRAIN / ERZBERGER'S COMPARTMENT - NIGHT 66
scenes.summary: Erzberger sits awake in his train compartment unable to sleep, picks up pen and paper to make notes, with a photo of his son on the nightstand.

scenes.heading: 67 INT. DIPLOMATIC TRAIN / HALL - NIGHT 67
scenes.summary: Erzberger leaves his compartment in pajamas and staggers down the hall to the toilet.

scenes.heading: 68 INT. DIPLOMATIC TRAIN / TOILET - NIGHT 68
scenes.summary: Erzberger rehearses diplomatic phrases in the toilet, the train jerks causing him to miss and curse, then he sees another train on parallel tracks through a window slit.

scenes.heading: 69 INT. DIPLOMATIC TRAIN / HALL - NIGHT 69
scenes.summary: Erzberger exits the toilet and encounters Captain von Helldorf in the hallway, telling him they have arrived at their destination.

scenes.heading: 70 EXT. STAGING CAMP - NIGHT 70
scenes.summary: Lieutenant Hoppe marches through the quiet staging camp in darkness toward the barracks.

scenes.heading: 71 INT. BARRACKS / STAGING AREA CAMP - NIGHT 71
scenes.summary: Lieutenant Hoppe wakes the soldiers by hitting bunk beds with a cane, screaming orders to pack and advance to the front line, threatening anyone not ready in fifteen minutes.

scenes.heading: 72 EXT. TROOP TRUCK / ROAD BEHIND THE FRONT - NIGHT 72
scenes.summary: A convoy of trucks carries crowded soldiers through the night as Kropp carves and plays a wooden flute he made for his sister.

scenes.heading: 73 OMITTED 73
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 74 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / ENTRY TO ACCESS TRENCH - FIRST LIGHT 74
scenes.summary: Soldiers jump into an access trench one by one in the blue-black dawn light illuminated by distant muzzle flashes and flares.

scenes.heading: 75 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / ENTRY TO ACCESS TRENCH - FIRST LIGHT 75
scenes.summary: The soldiers march in formation through the trench as Paul stares ahead, Müller wears his scarf proudly, and Kropp quietly plays his flute.

scenes.heading: 76 INT. DIPLOMATIC TRAIN - DUSK 76
scenes.summary: The German delegation sits in their converted train compartment meeting room at dawn, including Major General von Winterfeldt and other officials.

scenes.heading: 77 EXT. COMPIÈGNE WOODS - DUSK 77
scenes.summary: French guardsmen escort the German delegation through muddy woods between two trains, and Erzberger slips and gets his shoe stuck in mud.

scenes.heading: 78 INT. MARSHAL FOCH'S TRAIN - DUSK 78
scenes.summary: The German delegation enters Marshal Foch's remodeled dining car where the Allied delegates are seated at a long oak table with a draft treaty before them.
scenes.turningPointNote: The armistice negotiations begin, representing the political machinery that will determine the war's end while soldiers continue dying.

scenes.heading: 79 EXT. LATIERRE / TRENCH - DUSK 79
scenes.summary: Soldiers spread out in the foggy trench at dawn as Sergeant Steinberger observes enemy lines through periscope binoculars, a recruit vomits, and Kropp pins his theater poster to the trench wall.

scenes.heading: 80 INT/EXT. LATIERRE / TRENCH - DUSK 80
scenes.summary: Lieutenant Hoppe receives orders over a field phone, hangs up wordlessly, and heads out to the trench.

scenes.heading: 81 EXT. LATIERRE / TRENCH - DUSK 81
scenes.summary: Lieutenant Hoppe gives the order to march, Sergeant Steinberger passes it down the trench, and Paul clutches his rifle as the men prepare to climb out.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final major battle begins as the soldiers receive the order to attack, launching the climactic sequence while peace negotiations occur simultaneously.

scenes.heading: 82 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / NO MAN'S LAND - DUSK 82
scenes.summary: Paul and soldiers charge across No Man's Land toward enemy lines as machine guns open fire, fellow soldiers fall around him, and Lieutenant Hoppe is hit by an explosion.

scenes.heading: 83 EXT. LATIERRE / FRENCH TRENCH - DUSK 83
scenes.summary: A French machine gunner fires deadly salvos from the enemy trench.

scenes.heading: 84 EXT. LATIERRE / NO MAN'S LAND - DUSK 84
scenes.summary: Paul takes cover behind an earth berm, pulls a hand grenade pin, tosses it into the enemy trench killing French soldiers, then charges forward firing and screaming.

scenes.heading: 85 EXT. LATIERRE / FRENCH TRENCH - DUSK 85
scenes.summary: Paul shoots a fleeing Frenchman, stabs another with his bayonet, then kills a third attacker by repeatedly hitting him with a trench shovel until bones break.

scenes.heading: 86 INT. LATIERRE / FRENCH SHELTER / KITCHEN - DUSK 86
scenes.summary: Tjaden shoots three French soldiers in their kitchen shelter while Kat covers him, then they plunder supplies, eating corned beef and drinking cognac.

scenes.heading: 87 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / FRENCH TRENCH - DAY 87
scenes.summary: German soldiers line up in the captured trench when three French tanks emerge from the fog, rolling toward them with cannons and machine guns as hell breaks loose.

scenes.heading: 88 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / NO MAN'S LAND - DAY 88
scenes.summary: Paul places grenades on a tank's tracks, the explosion stops it, Kropp throws a grenade through the visor, and they shoot the burning crew members who emerge.

scenes.heading: 89 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / TRENCH - DAY 89
scenes.summary: Soldiers crowd around a machine gun's cooling water to drink thirstily while the gunner mows down advancing Frenchmen and a flame thrower tank explodes.

scenes.heading: 90 EXT. LATIERRE / NO MAN'S LAND - DAY 90
scenes.summary: German troops retreat across the battlefield as tanks burn, flame throwers belch fire, and French infantry advance.

scenes.heading: 91 EXT. DIPLOMATIC TRAIN - DAY 91
scenes.summary: A French guardsman smokes in the fog beside the deserted diplomatic train in the woods.

scenes.heading: 92 INT. DIPLOMATIC TRAIN - DAY 92
scenes.summary: The German delegation argues over the Allied treaty terms, with Major General Winterfeldt calling it a dictate rather than negotiation and Erzberger warning of continued casualties.

scenes.heading: 93 EXT. EGUISAC MANOR - DAY 93
scenes.summary: General Friedrichs steps onto his headquarters balcony and looks out at the distant battle with doubt shadowing his face as he resists the looming defeat.

scenes.heading: 94 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / HINTERLAND - DAY 94
scenes.summary: Paul carries a heavy tripod while retreating through a wasteland of blasted tree trunks and craters as shells explode nearby, killing a private, and bullets zing past.

scenes.heading: 95 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / CRATER - DAY 95
scenes.summary: Paul leaps into a crater filled with oily blood, hides in the stinking puddle as French soldiers pass overhead, then a Frenchman appears at the crater's edge and chambers a round.

scenes.heading: 96 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / HINTERLAND - DUSK 96
scenes.summary: Paul crawls out of the crater and stumbles through the deserted battlefield past corpses and a bunker, leaving behind a fluttering envelope with a printer's address.

scenes.heading: 97 INT. EGUISAC MANOR / DINING ROOM – NIGHT 97
scenes.summary: General Friedrichs and Major von Brixdorf dine on pigeon and red wine while discussing their birth years and family backgrounds.

scenes.heading: 98 INT. EGUISAC MANOR / STUDY - DUSK 98
scenes.summary: A boy tends to General Friedrichs' study, piling firewood, lighting a fire, emptying trash, and adjusting the desk chair in the last orderly section of the western front.

scenes.heading: 99 EXT. EGUISAC - DUSK 99
scenes.summary: Paul reaches the destroyed village of Eguisac with bombed-out roofs and soldiers warming themselves at campfires.

scenes.heading: 100 EXT. EQUISAC / VILLAGE SQUARE - NIGHT 100 100
scenes.summary: Soldiers drag mattresses into the village square, lie in a four-poster bed in the street, chase a pig, plunder houses, and kneel before a drunk priest giving blessings.

scenes.heading: 101 INT. EGUISAC INFIRMARY - NIGHT 101 101
scenes.summary: In a church converted to an infirmary, an army surgeon prepares to amputate a recruit's shattered arm with a bone saw while medics apply chloroform as Paul enters.

scenes.heading: 102 INT. EGUISAC INFIRMARY / HALL - NIGHT 102 102
scenes.summary: Paul walks through the crowded infirmary hall when Tjaden calls to him from outside.

scenes.heading: 103 EXT. EGUISAC INFIRMARY / YARD - NIGHT 103 103
scenes.summary: Paul finds Tjaden lying on a wooden bier with a shattered, gangrenous knee wound, and Tjaden speaks of peace coming soon while worrying he can't become a forest ranger now.
scenes.turningPointNote: Tjaden's severe injury and talk of impending peace represents the tragic irony of soldiers being destroyed just as the war is ending.

scenes.heading: 104 EXT. EGUISAC INFIRMARY - NIGHT 104 104
scenes.summary: Paul comes out onto the street, looks at the scarf, and ties it around his neck to wear permanently.

scenes.heading: 105 INT. EGUISAC SOUP KITCHEN - NIGHT 105 105
scenes.summary: At the soup kitchen, Kat confronts the Army Cook about serving food while Paul recognizes his voice and approaches with a mess kit.

scenes.heading: 106 EXT. EGUISAC INFIRMARY - NIGHT 106 106
scenes.summary: Kat and Paul carry their food back to the infirmary, joking about death and survival as they reach the church.

scenes.heading: 107 INT. EGUISAC INFIRMARY / YARD - NIGHT 107 107
scenes.summary: Kat wakes the injured Tjaden to give him beans while Paul avoids eye contact with a starving soldier watching them eat.

scenes.heading: 108 EXT. EQUISAC / FIELD - NIGHT 108 108
scenes.summary: A flare sails into the sky over reedy grass wafting in the wind.

scenes.heading: 109 INT/EXT. EGUISAC / VILLAGE SQUARE RESTAURANT - NIGHT 109 109
scenes.summary: Paul and Kat drink rum in a destroyed restaurant, with Paul searching desperately for Duval's address and expressing regret about not learning French.

scenes.heading: 110 INT. DIPLOMATIC TRAIN / ERZBERGER'S COMPARTMENT - NIGHT 110 110
scenes.summary: Erzberger cleans mud off his shoe while reading about the Kaiser's abdication, then receives a telegram from Von Helldorf about Hindenburg.

scenes.heading: 111 INT. MARSHAL FOCH'S TRAIN - NIGHT 111 111
scenes.summary: Marshal Foch tastes stale croissants before Erzberger arrives to announce the Kaiser's abdication and discuss the Armistice terms.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Armistice negotiations begin, marking the potential end of the war

scenes.heading: 112 EXT. EGUISAC MANOR - NIGHT 112 112
scenes.summary: Celebrating soldiers pass by the manor shooting in the air and singing as news of the Armistice spreads.

scenes.heading: 113 INT. EGUISAC MANOR / STUDY - NIGHT 113 113
scenes.summary: General Friedrichs watches the celebrating soldiers from his window while Major von Brixdorf brings news of the Armistice to German high command.

scenes.heading: 114 INT/EXT. EGUISAC / RESTAURANT ON VILLAGE SQUARE - DAWN 114 114
scenes.summary: A radio repeatedly broadcasts the Armistice terms while drunk soldiers sleep off their celebrations in the village square.

scenes.heading: 115 EXT. VALLEY / FARM YARD - DAY 115 115
scenes.summary: Kat and Paul cross a meadow discussing Christmas plans, with Kat describing his wife and dreams of having children again.

scenes.heading: 116 EXT. FARM YARD - DAY 116 116
scenes.summary: At the farm gate, Paul insists on going over the wall instead of Kat, who helps hoist him over while warning about the dog.

scenes.heading: 117 EXT. FARM YARD - DAY 117 117
scenes.summary: Paul sneaks to the barn, removes the wooden beam, and enters after hearing steps and flapping sounds inside.

scenes.heading: 118 INT. BARN - DAY 118 118
scenes.summary: Paul finds eggs in the dark barn but is discovered by a little boy, whom he tries to quiet by offering an egg before the boy runs off.

scenes.heading: 119 EXT. OUTSIDE FARM YARD - DAY 119 119
scenes.summary: Kat waits outside chewing tobacco when he hears the barn door slam and Paul calling for him to get out.

scenes.heading: 120 EXT. FARM YARD 120 120
scenes.summary: Paul runs from the barn as the angry farmer emerges with his son and rifle, with a dog barking somewhere.

scenes.heading: 121 INT. STABLE - DAY 121 121
scenes.summary: Paul rushes into the stable as the farmer shoots at him, the blast barely missing and shattering the door frame before Paul escapes.

scenes.heading: 122 EXT. FARM YARD - DAY 122 122
scenes.summary: Paul staggers out to meet Kat as the farmer reloads and fires again, the bullet hitting a branch as they escape out of range.

scenes.heading: 123 EXT. VALLEY / FARM YARD - DAY HIT 123 123
scenes.summary: Paul and Kat discover a bullet hole in Paul's pocket with broken egg dripping out, realizing how close the shot came to hitting him.

scenes.heading: EXT. VALLEY / EDGE OF WODDS - DAY 123A
scenes.summary: Kat urinates against a tree when the farm boy appears with a rifle, cocking it while Kat recognizes the boy's innocence and youth.

scenes.heading: EXT. VALLEY / FARM YARD - DAY 123B
scenes.summary: Paul sits examining broken eggshells when a gunshot from the woods makes him jump up and call for Kat.

scenes.heading: EXT. VALLEY / EDGE OF WODDS - DAY 123C
scenes.summary: Paul finds Kat leaning on a stump in the woods, with Kat limping and dismissing what happened while urging they leave.

scenes.heading: EXT. VALLEY WITH MANOR - DAY 123D
scenes.summary: Kat collapses on the meadow and reveals he was shot by the farm boy, with Paul discovering a liver wound oozing dark blood.
scenes.turningPointNote: Kat is mortally wounded, beginning Paul's final loss of his closest companion

scenes.heading: 124 EXT. EDGE OF WOODS BEI EGUISAC - DAY 124 124
scenes.summary: Paul carries the injured Kat on his back, trying unsuccessfully to stop passing trucks full of reinforcements for help.

scenes.heading: 125 EXT. EGUISAC / VILLAGE SQUARE - DAY 125 125
scenes.summary: Paul carries Kat through the crowd of excited recruits arriving on trucks in the village square.

scenes.heading: 126 INT. EGUISAC INFIRMARY - DAY 126 126
scenes.summary: Paul brings Kat to the deserted infirmary where a medic tells him Kat is already dead from organ poisoning.
scenes.turningPointNote: Kat's death is revealed, completing Paul's isolation and loss of his last meaningful connection

scenes.heading: 127 INT/EXT. EGUISAC / RESTAURANT ON VILLAGE SQUARE - DAY 127 127
scenes.summary: Paul sits alone in the destroyed restaurant holding Kat's dog tag while celebrating soldiers invite him to join them, but he remains isolated.

scenes.heading: 128 EXT. EGUISAC - DAY 128 128
scenes.summary: Young recruits no older than 17 arrive in trucks, with 16-year-old Walter dropping his helmet as Paul walks among them barely feeling his feet.

scenes.heading: 129 EXT. EGUISAC INFIRMARY - DAY 129 129
scenes.summary: Doctors, medics, and patients stream out of the infirmary as an MP drives them forward, with everyone in an upbeat mood.

scenes.heading: 130 INT. EGUISAC MANOR / ANTEROOM & STUDY - DAY 130 130
scenes.summary: Officers pack up the reception room frantically while General Friedrichs sits smoking in his office, watching the chaos with internal turmoil.

scenes.heading: 131 EXT. EGUISAC MANOR - DAY 131 131
scenes.summary: General Friedrichs addresses hundreds of assembled soldiers about enemies and betrayal, ordering them to attack the French positions before the Armistice takes effect.
scenes.turningPointNote: Friedrichs orders a final attack just before the Armistice, setting up the tragic final battle

scenes.heading: 132 INT. EGUISAC MANOR / STUDY - DAY 132 132
scenes.summary: General Friedrichs sits at his desk ignoring the gunshots from the yard while his dog briefly looks up then returns to sleep.

scenes.heading: 133 INT. EGUISAC SOUP KITCHEN - DAY 133 133
scenes.summary: Flies circle dried soup on the floor of the deserted, messy soup kitchen.

scenes.heading: 134 EXT. EGUISAC - DAY 134 134
scenes.summary: Soldiers stream out of the village trudging silently through mud and fog while officers and MPs follow on horseback with rifles and ammunition.

scenes.heading: 135 EXT. EDGE OF WOODS OUTSIDE EGUISAC - DAY 135 135
scenes.summary: MPs herd soldiers out of the fog toward the emplacements at the edge of the woods.

scenes.heading: 136 INT. EGUISAC INFIRMARY - DAY 136 136
scenes.summary: The infirmary is deserted except for one nurse hurrying past the wounded men who remain behind.

scenes.heading: 137 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / EMPLACEMENTS - DAY 137 137
scenes.summary: Paul advances with soldiers until they halt at the hill crest overlooking fog-covered plains, then wordlessly fix bayonets.

scenes.heading: 138 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / TRENCH - DAY 138 138
scenes.summary: French Major Grenier talks with soldiers on the latrine about collecting medals and dead comrades before encountering Corporal Moreau in the trench.

scenes.heading: 139 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / EMPLACEMENTS - DAY 139 139
scenes.summary: A young German soldier checks his watch showing fifteen minutes remaining while Paul stares at the battlefield and the MP signals the advance.

scenes.heading: 140 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / TRENCH - DAY 140 140
scenes.summary: French soldiers relax in their positions playing cards and sharing cognac, with peace just minutes away as they joke and laugh together.

scenes.heading: 141 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / HINTERLAND - DAY 141 141
scenes.summary: German soldiers break into a run toward French lines through craters and blasted trees, with Paul stony-faced among them carrying rifle and battle gear.

scenes.heading: 142 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / TRENCH - DAY 142 142
scenes.summary: Major Grenier tastes soup when he hears distant marching sounds, climbs up to peer into No Man's Land, and calls out to his missing soldiers.

scenes.heading: 143 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / HINTERLAND - DAY 143 143
scenes.summary: Paul races forward with the infantry as whistles sound and men scream like berserkers while the first machine gun salvo hits the mud.

scenes.heading: 144 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / TRENCH - DAY 144 144
scenes.summary: French soldiers are caught off guard as Major Grenier blows his whistle and they frantically feed ammunition belts while German soldiers emerge from the fog.

scenes.heading: 145 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / HINTERLAND - DAY 145 145
scenes.summary: Machine gun fire cuts through German ranks as Paul takes cover behind a dead horse while soldiers slip in mud and a grenade explosion tears apart a nearby soldier.

scenes.heading: 146 EXT. LATIERRE / FRENCH TRENCH - DAY 146 146
scenes.summary: Major Grenier orders a charge as the French jump from their destroyed trench to engage in hand-to-hand combat.

scenes.heading: 147 EXT. LATIERRE / HINTERLAND - DAY 147 147
scenes.summary: Paul shoots at attacking Frenchmen before engaging in brutal hand-to-hand combat, losing his rifle and using his helmet to strike an enemy in close quarters fighting.

scenes.heading: 148 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / TRENCH - DAY 148 148
scenes.summary: Paul wrestles with a French soldier in the trench, strangling and fighting in brutal close combat until they both fall into a bunker below.

scenes.heading: 149 INT. LATIERRE HILL / BUNKER - DAY 149 149
scenes.summary: Paul lands hard and faces his enemy when a young French soldier charges from the shadows and stabs Paul in the back with a bayonet, with the blade protruding from his chest.
scenes.turningPointNote: Paul is fatally wounded by a bayonet thrust, marking his death in the final moments before the Armistice

scenes.heading: 150 EXT. LATIERRE HILL / TRENCH - DAY 150 150
scenes.summary: Officers pass the cease fire order at eleven o'clock, gunfire stops creating eerie silence, and Paul sinks to the ground with blood gushing from his wound while holding Franz's scarf.
scenes.turningPointNote: The war officially ends with the ceasefire, but Paul is mortally wounded in the final moments

scenes.heading: 151 INT. MANOR / STUDY - DAY 151 151
scenes.summary: General Friedrichs sits stonily in his study as the grandfather clock strikes eleven, then hangs his head in the quiet aftermath.

scenes.heading: 152 EXT. LATIERRE / TRENCH - DAY 152 152
scenes.summary: Dead bodies lie across the silent battlefield as surviving soldiers crawl from craters and stagger homeward, with Major Grenier helping a wounded soldier.

scenes.heading: 153 EXT. LATIERRE / TRENCH - DAY 153 153
scenes.summary: Major von Brixdorf orders Walter to collect dog tags from the dead, and Walter discovers Paul Bäumer's body sitting composed against the trench wall.
scenes.turningPointNote: The protagonist Paul's death is revealed, completing his tragic arc from idealistic student to war casualty
